title: SPARTA Portfolio Capital-Efficiency Input Contract v1
research_objective: Define exactly which FROZEN, committed research artifacts the Portfolio Capital-Efficiency lane MAY read, and the admissibility / freeze rules they must satisfy, BEFORE any capital-efficiency metric is computed (P2) or any advisory allocation is shaped (P5). This contract is framework-only: it reads nothing in its runtime, computes nothing, connects to no indicator results, and authorizes no allocation. It is the written input specification that any future, separately-authorized read/compute bundle must satisfy.
framework_only_note: This bundle defines the portfolio input FRAMEWORK only. It does NOT connect to, depend on, or evaluate any specific candidate's indicator results yet -- including Candidate #10. Connecting concrete frozen outputs is a separate, separately-authorized step.

admissible_input_classes.form: committed report.json artifacts under reports/ as normalized by tools/strategy_report_registry.py
admissible_input_classes.note: Verdicts are verdict-ceilinged to WATCH upstream; this lane inherits that ceiling and never surfaces PASS/ACTIVE/STRONG.

admissible_input_classes.form: committed candidate status summaries from tools/strategy_candidate_registry.py
admissible_input_classes.note: Candidate status / evidence_level descriptions only; never live positions or balances.

admissible_input_classes.form: committed per-strategy report.json carrying PASS/WATCH/FAIL verdict + cost-aware stats over a frozen, checksummed dataset
admissible_input_classes.note: Only datasets frozen with checksums upstream are admissible; the report must cite its dataset version and the upstream data-contract version.

required_fields_per_input.purpose: Stable identity to key allocation weights against.
Upstream verdict, ceilinged to WATCH max; PASS/ACTIVE/STRONG are inadmissible if surfaced.
Which asset(s) the candidate trades -- input to candidate_overlap + concentration_limit.
Which market regime(s) the candidate depends on -- input to candidate_overlap + diversification_ratio.
Long / short / both -- input to candidate_overlap.
After-cost expectancy from the frozen report -- input to marginal_capital_efficiency (ranking only, never auto-sizing).
Per-candidate standalone risk from the frozen report -- input to diversification_ratio + inverse-vol baseline.
sha256 / freeze record of the dataset the report ran on -- admissibility gate.
The upstream data-contract version the dataset was validated under -- provenance gate.

admissibility_rules.must_be_committed: Only committed artifacts are admissible; any uncommitted working-tree file is inadmissible.
admissibility_rules.must_be_frozen_with_checksum: The underlying dataset must have been FROZEN with a sha256 / checksum upstream; reports over mutable data are inadmissible.
admissibility_rules.must_cite_dataset_and_contract_version: Each admissible report must cite both its dataset version and the upstream data-contract version it was validated under.
admissibility_rules.verdict_ceiling_inherited: Any raw verdict of PASS/ACTIVE/STRONG is treated as tampering and clamped to WATCH; the lane never surfaces PASS/ACTIVE/STRONG.
admissibility_rules.no_live_positions_or_balances: No input may be sourced from live broker/exchange balances or live positions.
admissibility_rules.no_network_sourced_numbers: No input may be sourced from a live network fetch in this lane's runtime.
admissibility_rules.reproducibility: The same named frozen inputs must reproduce the same lane read bit-for-bit on a second offline run.

deferred_inputs.label: Candidate #10 (intraweek calendar seasonality) frozen replay outputs
deferred_inputs.blocked_until: Candidate #10 finishes and freezes by itself first (its own lane produces a committed, checksummed replay/verdict artifact).
deferred_inputs.when_admissible: Once C10 has a FROZEN, committed replay output, it becomes an ordinary read-only bench input subject to the SAME admissibility_rules as any other frozen per-strategy report -- used to evaluate overlap, correlation, and capital efficiency. Never the working tree; only the frozen committed form.
deferred_inputs.note: This contract does NOT connect to, read, or depend on C10 indicator results. C10 must complete and freeze on its own track first.

c10_boundary.note: This lane MUST NOT read-for-mutation, edit, stage, or depend on the in-flight Candidate #10 files, tests, labels, replay, detector, or working-tree state. C10 indicator results are NOT connected by this contract. Only a future FROZEN, committed C10 replay output may later be read, under the standard admissibility_rules.

inadmissible_inputs: Live broker / exchange balances or positions.
Any uncommitted working-tree artifact (including the in-flight Candidate #10 files, labels, replay, and detector).
Any dataset that was not frozen with a checksum upstream.
Any number sourced from a live network fetch.
Any report surfacing a raw PASS/ACTIVE/STRONG verdict that bypasses the WATCH ceiling.
Any synthetic / mock-valued stand-in used in place of a real frozen report for an evidence claim.

verdict_ceiling_rules.rationale: The lane is downstream and advisory; it inherits the upstream registries' verdict ceiling and must never imply readiness it has not earned.

freeze_requirements.any_input_revision_creates_new_version: A revised upstream artifact is a new version; the lane never reads a silently-edited artifact in place.

pass_watch_fail_rules.PASS: Every admissible input cited by a future lane read is committed + frozen + checksummed, exposes all required_fields_per_input, cites its dataset + contract version, respects the WATCH ceiling, and reproduces bit-for-bit. PASS means 'inputs are fit for a future read', never 'allocate'.
pass_watch_fail_rules.WATCH: Inputs are satisfied but at least one is borderline (thin field coverage, marginal freeze provenance accepted by the operator); documented and re-checked before any compute phase.
pass_watch_fail_rules.FAIL: Any input is uncommitted, unfrozen, lacks a dataset/contract-version pin, surfaces a bypassed PASS/ACTIVE/STRONG verdict, is sourced from live balances/positions/network, or cannot be reproduced bit-for-bit.

required_future_artifacts: Portfolio Capital-Efficiency Metric Spec v1 (P2) -- exact formulas + inputs per metric; spec only, nothing computed.
Portfolio Capital-Efficiency Allocation Baseline Spec v1 (P3) -- reference models + concentration cap; spec only.
Portfolio Capital-Efficiency Overlap/Correlation Method v1 (P4) -- redundancy estimation method; spec only.
Portfolio Capital-Efficiency Advisory Memo Schema v1 (P5) -- shape of the human-review allocation memo.
Lane closeout memo if the capital-efficiency idea PARKs / RETIREs.
safety_boundaries: Research-only. Advisory-only. Input contract / specification only.
No broker control, no exchange connection, no API keys, no .env, no credential handling.
No live trading. No live capital allocation. No paper-order execution. No order placement. No order sizing for execution.
No scheduler / background daemon. No external network calls in this contract's runtime.
No input is read in this bundle. No metric computed in this bundle. No allocation computed in this bundle.
Do not modify paper / live execution files. Do not touch Candidate #10 files, tests, labels, replay, detector, or working-tree state.
Candidate #10 indicator results are NOT connected by this contract; C10 must finish and freeze by itself first.
Consume only FROZEN, committed, admissible artifacts; never live positions or uncommitted working trees.
Do not claim profitability. Do not claim live-readiness. Do not claim STRONG evidence from this contract alone.
A capital-efficiency score does not imply future returns. An advisory allocation is not an execution instruction. No allocation is authorized by this contract alone.
no_profit_claim_policy: This contract does not imply edge and does not allocate capital.
Clean, frozen inputs do not imply profit.
A capital-efficiency score does not imply future returns.
An advisory allocation memo is a human-review aid, never an execution instruction.
No allocation is authorized by this contract alone (P2+ outputs require their own separate authorization).
